---
title: "QGIS 4.0 Norrköping"
author:
  - name: Ralph Straumann
    url: https://ralphstraumann.ch
date: "2026-03-10 19:53"
image: "qgis-4.jpg"
description: 'QGIS 4.0 "Norrköping" has arrived – familiar on the surface, but rebuilt on Qt6 with over 100 new features, improved performance, and a stronger foundation for the future.'
---

On March 6, [QGIS version 4.0.0][qgis4], codenamed "[Norrköping][norrköping]", 
was [released][released]. From the announcement: 

> On the surface, existing users should expect to engage with a GIS experience 
familiar to what they have come to know and love from previous QGIS releases. 
Under the hood, however, it introduces significant changes to maintainability 
and usability. These changes ensure that QGIS 4.0 can unlock additional access 
to modern libraries while bringing much-needed performance and security 
improvements to the codebase.
>
> (...)
>
> While preparation for the QGIS 4.0 migration has been underway, the developer 
community has added over 100 new features across the application, making QGIS 
more powerful, more flexible, more secure, and generally just more awesome. 

In a major update to the underlying application framework, QGIS 4 is built on 
[Qt6][qt6]. 

As to other improvements, some highlights are listed on the [release 
page][released] (scroll down). And there is a longish (23 minutes) 
"visual changelog", essentially a video showing new features:

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wTK1exokBA8 >}}

[qgis4]: https://qgis.org/download/
[norrköping]: https://duckduckgo.com/?q=Norrk%C3%B6ping&iaxm=maps
[released]: https://changelog.qgis.org/en/version/4.0/
[qt6]: https://www.qt.io/development/qt-framework/qt6
